title: Reti
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type=Criminal |image=[[File:Reti-.jpg]] |name=Reti |homeworld=[[Toydaria/Legends|Toydaria]] |birth= |death= |species=[[Toydarian/Legends|Toydarian]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height=1.3 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes=[[Brown]] |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=[[Lok Revenants/Legends|Lok Revenants]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Reti! I thought you were dead.''"
"''When did you get so pessimistic?|[[Vana Sage]], to Reti|[[Star Wars: Starfighter (video game)|''Star Wars: Starfighter'']]}} '''Reti''' was an overly talkative [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Toydarian/Legends|Toydarian]] mechanic who operated during the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]].[[Star Wars: Starfighter (video game)|''Star Wars: Starfighter'']] He later joined the [[Lok Revenants/Legends|Lok Revenants]], under the leadership of the pirate chief [[Nym/Legends|Nym]].[[Star Wars: Starfighter (video game)|''Star Wars: Starfighter'']] Mission 1: The Informant ==Biography== {{Quote|Reti, have you ever considered a career in piracy?''"
"''Is that a job offer? I don't work cheap you know!|[[Nym/Legends|Nym]] and Reti|}} Reti made his living as a [[Scavenger/Legends|salvage]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]], flying his personal vessel, the ''[[Zoomer]]'', in and around the [[Naboo system/Legends|Naboo]] and [[Karthakk system]]s. Reti was known for his motor-mouth, and liked to brag that he downed sixteen [[Droid starfighter/Legends|droid starfighters]] during the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]]. In reality, Reti was a skittish and cowardly creature, though was an able fighter pilot at the controls of the ''Zoomer''. Reti had a number of contacts across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], including the mercenary [[Vana Sage]] and a particularly ornery Toydarian [[Watto/Legends|junk dealer]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]].[[Star Wars: Starfighter (video game)|''Star Wars: Starfighter'']] Mission 3: Prison Break At some point in his past, Reti operated as a [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] on [[Oovo IV/Legends|Oovo IV]]. Reti assisted the [[Bravo Squadron/Legends|Bravo Squadron]] pilot [[Rhys Dallows]] prior to the Battle of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] when the young cadet's starfighter was damaged by a mercenary squadron in the [[Chommell sector/Legends|Chommell sector]]. Reti towed Dallows' fighter to Vana Sage's outpost, and later accompanied the pilots during sorties against forces of the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]]. He also flew alongside Nym, and fell in with his Revenants following the Battle of Naboo. For the next ten years, Reti continued to ply his trade, and remained in contact with Nym, whom Reti counted as an old friend; at one point, Nym had to bail Reti out when the Toydarian ran into a "nasty group of mercenaries" known as the [[Sabaoth Squadron/Legends|Sabaoth Squadron]]. When [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Adi Gallia/Legends|Adi Gallia]] arranged a meeting with Nym to discuss Trade Federation activity in the Karthakk system, Reti was sent to meet the Jedi in the [[Ruby Nebula]]. Unfortunately, and despite Reti's protests to the contrary, the salvager was tailed by a [[C-9979 landing craft/Legends|Trade Federation landing craft]], though Jedi and Toydarian worked together to destroy the vessel. Reti led Gallia to Nym on [[Maramere]], and—despite his boss's reservations about the presence of a Jedi—about both helped the pirate liberate his troops from the infamous [[Spacestation 1138]]. Reti accompanied Adi Gallia on several more missions, culminating in an assault against the fortified world of [[Nod Kartha]], home of a large [[trihexalon]] factory. Nym's forces were ambushed by the [[Sabaoth Squadron/Legends|Sabaoth Squadron]], but worse, the deadly [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] emerged from [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] in the [[Slave I/Legends|''Slave I'']] to claim a bounty on Reti's head. Dodging laser blasts, Reti had no choice but to fire his hyperdrive in a wild jump to flee the relentless [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]]. His colleagues could do nothing to help, and remained unaware of Reti's final destination.[[Star Wars: Starfighter (video game)|''Star Wars: Starfighter'']] Mission 7: Hammer and Anvil Following the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|First Battle of Geonosis]], in which Nym's forces battled [[Cavik Toth]]'s fleet above the planet, Vana Sage embarked on a mission to locate Reti—or learn whatever fate had befallen the hapless Toydarian.[[Star Wars: Starfighter (video game)|''Star Wars: Starfighter'']] Mission 15: The Jedi Master ==Personality and traits== Reti was able to read and write in both [[Toydarian (language)/Legends|Toydarian]] and [[Huttese/Legends|Huttese]]. He could also speak [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]], Huttese, [[Pak Pak/Legends|Pak Pak]], [[Twi'leki/Legends|Ryl]], and Toydarian.{{Wizards|url=starwars/article.asp?x=sw20011115c_reti&c=rpg|text=Meet Reti, Salvage Pilot}} ==Behind the scenes== Reti was voiced in both [[Star Wars: Starfighter (video game)|''Star Wars: Starfighter'']] and ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Starfighter]]'' by [[Wally Wingert]]. In the Prison Break mission of ''Star Wars: Jedi Starfighter'', upon destroying several frigates, Reti comments that a "Toydarian on Tatooine" would "pay good credits for this junk." This is apparently intended as a reference to Watto, though as [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']] shows, Watto refuses to deal in Republic credits. However, Reti's use of the term "credits" could be a slang, generic term for currency, much like the real-world term "cash," which can refer to money in any form. ==Appearances== *[[Star Wars: Starfighter (video game)|''Star Wars: Starfighter'']] {{1st}} *''[[Star Wars: Racer Revenge]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[Star Wars: Jedi Starfighter]]'' ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Reti}} *[[Star Wars: Starfighter: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Starfighter'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] *{{Wizards|url=starwars/article.asp?x=sw20011115c_reti&c=rpg|text=Meet Reti, Salvage Pilot}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[Galaxy of Intrigue]]'' *{{DB|character|reti|Reti}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Mechanics]] [[Category:Pirates]] [[Category:Resistance fighters]] [[Category:Scavengers]] [[Category:Toydarians]]
